2606.26964.title: Look-Before-Move: Narrative-Grounded World Visual Attention in Dynamic 3D Story Worlds
2606.26964.paper_summary: As embodied AI and world models increasingly operate in dynamic 3D environments, visual perception must move beyond passively interpreting given observations toward actively deciding what to observe. We study this problem through camera planning in dynamic 3D story worlds, where the camera must not only generate smooth motion, but also decide what visual evidence should be acquired before it moves. We formulate this capability as Narrative-Grounded World Visual Attention, where the camera acts as an embodied observer that determines what to observe, how to compose the observation, and how to shift attention over time under narrative intent and physical 3D constraints. To realize this capability, we propose Look-Before-Move, a camera planning framework that separates observation specification from motion execution. It first builds a Semantic Observation Contract to convert directorial intent into executable visual constraints, then performs Monte Carlo Viewpoint Search to find narrative-compliant and geometrically feasible viewpoints, and finally applies Semantic Trajectory Grounding to connect selected viewpoints into continuous, collision-aware, and temporally coherent camera motion. We further construct a dynamic 3D Story World Benchmark based on StoryBlender, covering 50 stories, 457 scenes, and 1585 shots with animated characters, semantic scene configurations, and executable 3D environments. Experiments show that our framework improves subject perception, intent consistency, and trajectory quality over representative baselines, demonstrating the importance of organizing visual attention before generating camera motion.
2606.26964.paper_summary_zh: 隨著具身 AI 和世界模型越來越多地在動態 3D 環境中運作，視覺感知必須超越被動地解釋給定的觀察，轉向主動決定觀察什麼。我們通過在動態 3D 故事世界中的相機規劃來研究這個問題，在這裡，相機不僅必須產生平滑的運動，還必須決定在移動之前應該獲取什麼視覺證據。我們將這種能力定義為敘事基礎的世界視覺注意力，其中相機作為一個具身的觀察者，決定觀察什麼、如何組合觀察，以及如何在敘事意圖和物理 3D 約束下隨時間轉移注意力。為了實現這種能力，我們提出了 Look-Before-Move，一個將觀察規範與運動執行分開的相機規劃框架。它首先建立語義觀察合約，將導演意圖轉換為可執行的視覺約束，然後執行蒙特卡羅視點搜索，以尋找符合敘事的幾何可行視點，最後應用語義軌跡基礎將選定的視點連接成連續的、考慮碰撞的和時間上連貫的相機運動。我們進一步基於 StoryBlender 構建了一個動態 3D 故事世界基準，涵蓋 50 個故事、457 個場景和 1585 個鏡頭，並包含動畫角色、語義場景配置和可執行的 3D 環境。實驗表明，我們的框架在主觀感知、意圖一致性和軌跡質量上優於代表性基準，顯示了在生成相機運動之前組織視覺注意力的重要性。
2606.26964.author: Jiaming Bian et.al.
2606.26964.authors: Jiaming Bian, Bingliang Li, Yuehao Wu, Pichao Wang, Zhi Wang, Hailan Ma, Huadong Mo, Zhenhong Sun
